title: Code Review: Add login endpoint with JWT auth

prContext.title: Add login endpoint with JWT auth
prContext.description: First-pass implementation of POST /login that authenticates users and issues JWT tokens. Closes #42.
prContext.author: Developer

scenes.narration: This PR adds a new login endpoint to authenticate users and issue JWT tokens. Let's see what the council found.

annotation.message: New authentication route added to Express app

scenes.narration: The new auth route creates its own database connection pool. This will cause problems at scale.

annotation.message: Per-route connection pool will exhaust resources

scenes.narration: Here's the login endpoint. Notice line 51 where the email is concatenated directly into the SQL query.

annotation.message: SQL injection vulnerability - user input concatenated into query

scenes.narration: An attacker could send a malicious email like: admin' OR '1'='1 to bypass authentication entirely.

annotation.message: SQL injection allows arbitrary database access

scenes.narration: Line 61 compares passwords in plaintext. User passwords should never be stored or compared without hashing.

annotation.message: Plaintext password comparison - use bcrypt or argon2

scenes.narration: Line 70 hardcodes the JWT secret as supersecret123. Anyone with source access can forge authentication tokens.

annotation.message: Hardcoded JWT secret - move to environment variables

scenes.narration: The error handler on line 74 provides no logging or context. Production debugging will be impossible.

annotation.message: Zero observability - no logging for errors or auth attempts

scenes.narration: The council voted 3 to 2 to block this PR. Three critical security vulnerabilities make this unsuitable for production.

annotation.message: Multiple critical issues: SQL injection, plaintext passwords, hardcoded secrets

verdict.summary: Three critical security vulnerabilities create immediate exploit vectors: SQL injection allows arbitrary database queries, plaintext password comparison exposes credentials, and hardcoded JWT secret allows token forgery. Additionally, zero observability and per-route connection pools create operational risks. Required fixes: use parameterized queries, implement bcrypt hashing, move secrets to environment variables, add logging, and use shared connection pool.
